visualization.title: Number of course sections per professor

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT p.id AS professor_id,
       (p.first_name || ' ' || p.last_name) AS professor_name,
       COUNT(cs.id) AS section_count
FROM public.professors AS p
LEFT JOIN public.course_sections AS cs
  ON cs.professor_id = p.id
GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
ORDER BY section_count DESC, professor_name ASC;

issues: Professor display name (professor_name) may not be unique — consider using professor_id as a key or including the ID in axis labels to avoid ambiguity (ID is present in tooltip).
The x-axis sort uses op:'sum' on section_count which is redundant because section_count is already an aggregated value; harmless but unnecessary.

visualization.title: Number of course sections per professor

result.sql:
SELECT p.id AS professor_id,
       (p.first_name || ' ' || p.last_name) AS professor_name,
       COUNT(cs.id) AS section_count
FROM public.professors AS p
LEFT JOIN public.course_sections AS cs
  ON cs.professor_id = p.id
GROUP BY p.id, p.first_name, p.last_name
ORDER BY section_count DESC, professor_name ASC;

The x-axis sort uses op:'sum' on section_count which is redundant because section_count is already an aggregated value; harmless but unnecessary.
